   179→        "content": "     1→package transflow\n     2→\n     3→import (\n     4→\t\"context\"\n     5→\t\"encoding/json\"\n     6→\t\"fmt\"\n     7→\t\"time\"\n     8→)\n     9→\n    10→// Implementation of job submission helpers for the transflow healing workflow.\n    11→// This provides the GREEN phase implementation for the failing tests.\n    12→\n    13→// jobSubmissionHelper implements the JobSubmissionHelper interface\n    14→type jobSubmissionHelper struct {\n    15→\tsubmitter interface{} // MockJobSubmitter in tests, real submitter in production\n    16→}\n    17→\n    18→// NewJobSubmissionHelper creates a new job submission helper\n    19→func NewJobSubmissionHelper(submitter interface{}) JobSubmissionHelper {\n    20→\treturn &jobSubmissionHelper{\n    21→\t\tsubmitter: submitter,\n    22→\t}\n    23→}\n    24→\n    25→// SubmitPlannerJob submits a planner job after a build failure\n    26→func (h *jobSubmissionHelper) SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error) {\n    27→\t// Use type assertion to check if this is a test submitter\n    28→\t// In production, would use a different interface/implementation\n    29→\tif testSubmitter, ok := h.submitter.(interface {\n    30→\t\tSubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx context.Context, spec JobSpec) (JobResult, error)\n    31→\t}); ok {\n    32→\t\tspec := JobSpec{\n    33→\t\t\tName:    \"planner\",\n    34→\t\t\tType:    \"planner\",\n    35→\t\t\tHCLPath: \"\", // Would be set from rendered assets\n    36→\t\t\tEnvVars: map[string]string{\n    37→\t\t\t\t\"BUILD_ERROR\": buildError,\n    38→\t\t\t\t\"TARGET_REPO\": config.TargetRepo,\n    39→\t\t\t\t\"BASE_REF\":    config.BaseRef,\n    40→\t\t\t},\n    41→\t\t\tTimeout: 15 * time.Minute,\n    42→\t\t\tInputs: map[string]interface{}{\n    43→\t\t\t\t\"workspace\": workspace,\n    44→\t\t\t},\n    45→\t\t}\n    46→\n    47→\t\tresult, err := testSubmitter.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx, spec)\n    48→\t\tif err != nil {\n    49→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"planner job failed: %w\", err)\n    50→\t\t}\n    51→\n    52→\t\t// Parse the planner output\n    53→\t\tvar planResult PlanResult\n    54→\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(result.Output), &planResult); err != nil {\n    55→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse planner output: %w\", err)\n    56→\t\t}\n    57→\n    58→\t\treturn &planResult, nil\n    59→\t}\n    60→\n    61→\t// For production implementation, would use actual job submission\n    62→\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"production job submission not implemented yet\")\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→// SubmitReducerJob submits a reducer job to determine the next action\n    66→func (h *jobSubmissionHelper) SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error) {\n    67→\t// Use type assertion to check if this is a test submitter\n    68→\tif testSubmitter, ok := h.submitter.(interface {\n    69→\t\tSubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx context.Context, spec JobSpec) (JobResult, error)\n    70→\t}); ok {\n    71→\t\tspec := JobSpec{\n    72→\t\t\tName:    \"reducer\",\n    73→\t\t\tType:    \"reducer\",\n    74→\t\t\tHCLPath: \"\", // Would be set from rendered assets\n    75→\t\t\tEnvVars: map[string]string{\n    76→\t\t\t\t\"PLAN_ID\": planID,\n    77→\t\t\t},\n    78→\t\t\tTimeout: 10 * time.Minute,\n    79→\t\t\tInputs: map[string]interface{}{\n    80→\t\t\t\t\"workspace\": workspace,\n    81→\t\t\t\t\"results\":   results,\n    82→\t\t\t\t\"winner\":    winner,\n    83→\t\t\t},\n    84→\t\t}\n    85→\n    86→\t\tresult, err := testSubmitter.SubmitAndWaitTerminal(ctx, spec)\n    87→\t\tif err != nil {\n    88→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"reducer job failed: %w\", err)\n    89→\t\t}\n    90→\n    91→\t\t// Parse the reducer output\n    92→\t\tvar nextAction NextAction\n    93→\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(result.Output), &nextAction); err != nil {\n    94→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse reducer output: %w\", err)\n    95→\t\t}\n    96→\n    97→\t\treturn &nextAction, nil\n    98→\t}\n    99→\n   100→\t// For production implementation, would use actual job submission\n   101→\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"production job submission not implemented yet\")\n   102→}\n   103→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

   213→        "content": "     1→package orchestration\n     2→\n     3→import (\n     4→    \"fmt\"\n     5→    \"os\"\n     6→    \"time\"\n     7→\n     8→    nomadapi \"github.com/hashicorp/nomad/api\"\n     9→    \"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/utils\"\n    10→)\n    11→\n    12→// Submit reads an HCL job file, parses and registers it via Nomad API.\n    13→func Submit(jobPath string) error {\n    14→    hcl, err := os.ReadFile(jobPath)\n    15→    if err != nil {\n    16→        return fmt.Errorf(\"read job file: %w\", err)\n    17→    }\n    18→    client, err := newNomadClient()\n    19→    if err != nil {\n    20→        return err\n    21→    }\n    22→    jobs := client.Jobs()\n    23→    job, err := jobs.ParseHCL(string(hcl), true)\n    24→    if err != nil {\n    25→        return fmt.Errorf(\"parse HCL: %w\", err)\n    26→    }\n    27→    _, _, err = jobs.Register(job, nil)\n    28→    if err != nil {\n    29→        return fmt.Errorf(\"register job: %w\", err)\n    30→    }\n    31→    return nil\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→// SubmitAndWaitHealthy parses, registers the job, and waits for min healthy allocations.\n    35→func SubmitAndWaitHealthy(jobPath string, expectedCount int, timeout time.Duration) error {\n    36→    hcl, err := os.ReadFile(jobPath)\n    37→    if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"read job file: %w\", err) }\n    38→    client, err := newNomadClient()\n    39→    if err != nil { return err }\n    40→    jobs := client.Jobs()\n    41→    job, err := jobs.ParseHCL(string(hcl), true)\n    42→    if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"parse HCL: %w\", err) }\n    43→    if _, _, err := jobs.Register(job, nil); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"register job: %w\", err) }\n    44→    name := \"\"\n    45→    if job != nil && job.Name != nil { name = *job.Name }\n    46→    if name == \"\" { return nil }\n    47→    monitor := NewHealthMonitor()\n    48→    return monitor.WaitForHealthyAllocations(name, expectedCount, timeout)\n    49→}\n    50→\n    51→// ValidateJob parses HCL to validate syntax; returns error if invalid.\n    52→func ValidateJob(jobPath string) error {\n    53→    hcl, err := os.ReadFile(jobPath)\n    54→    if err != nil { return err }\n    55→    client, err := newNomadClient()\n    56→    if err != nil { return err }\n    57→    if _, err := client.Jobs().ParseHCL(string(hcl), true); err != nil {\n    58→        return fmt.Errorf(\"job parse/validate failed: %w\", err)\n    59→    }\n    60→    return nil\n    61→}\n    62→\n    63→// PlanJob is not implemented in SDK mode; returns a placeholder message.\n    64→func PlanJob(jobPath string) (string, error) {\n    65→    return \"plan not implemented in SDK mode\", nil\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→// StreamJobLogs is not implemented without Nomad client log streaming; returns error.\n    69→func StreamJobLogs(jobID string, follow bool) error {\n    70→    return fmt.Errorf(\"log streaming not implemented in orchestration SDK mode\")\n    71→}\n    72→\n    73→// WaitHealthy waits until a job has at least one healthy allocation or timeout.\n    74→func WaitHealthy(jobName string, timeout time.Duration) error {\n    75→    monitor := NewHealthMonitor()\n    76→    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)\n    77→    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {\n    78→        if monitor.IsJobHealthy(jobName) {\n    79→            return nil\n    80→        }\n    81→        time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)\n    82→    }\n    83→    return fmt.Errorf(\"timeout waiting for job %s to be healthy\", jobName)\n    84→}\n    85→\n    86→// SubmitAndWaitTerminal registers a batch job and waits until its allocations reach a terminal state\n    87→// (complete or failed), or the timeout elapses. This is intended for short-lived planner/reducer jobs.\n    88→func SubmitAndWaitTerminal(jobPath string, timeout time.Duration) error {\n    89→    hcl, err := os.ReadFile(jobPath)\n    90→    if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"read job file: %w\", err) }\n    91→    client, err := newNomadClient()\n    92→    if err != nil { return err }\n    93→    jobs := client.Jobs()\n    94→    job, err := jobs.ParseHCL(string(hcl), true)\n    95→    if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"parse HCL: %w\", err) }\n    96→    if _, _, err := jobs.Register(job, nil); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf(\"register job: %w\", err) }\n    97→    name := \"\"\n    98→    if job != nil && job.Name != nil { name = *job.Name }\n    99→    if name == \"\" { return fmt.Errorf(\"job name not resolved\") }\n   100→\n   101→    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)\n   102→    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {\n   103→        // List allocations for the job\n   104→        allocs, _, err := client.Jobs().Allocations(name, false, nil)\n   105→        if err != nil {\n   106→            // Non-fatal: wait and retry\n   107→            time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)\n   108→            continue\n   109→        }\n   110→        // Evaluate terminal states\n   111→        sawRunning := false\n   112→        for _, a := range allocs {\n   113→            cs := a.ClientStatus\n   114→            if cs == \"complete\" {\n   115→                return nil\n   116→            }\n   117→            if cs == \"failed\" {\n   118→                return fmt.Errorf(\"job %s allocation failed (%s)\", name, a.ID)\n   119→            }\n   120→            if cs == \"running\" || cs == \"pending\" || cs == \"starting\" {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

   247→        "content": "   120→            if cs == \"running\" || cs == \"pending\" || cs == \"starting\" {\n   121→                sawRunning = true\n   122→            }\n   123→        }\n   124→        if !sawRunning && len(allocs) > 0 {\n   125→            // Allocations exist but none running and none complete/failed; give them time\n   126→        }\n   127→        time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)\n   128→    }\n   129→    return fmt.Errorf(\"timeout waiting for job %s to complete\", name)\n   130→}\n   131→\n   132→func newNomadClient() (*nomadapi.Client, error) {\n   133→    cfg := nomadapi.DefaultConfig()\n   134→    if addr := utils.Getenv(\"NOMAD_ADDR\", \"\"); addr != \"\" {\n   135→        cfg.Address = addr\n   136→    }\n   137→    return nomadapi.NewClient(cfg)\n   138→}\n   139→\n   140→// DeregisterJob deregisters a job by name; if purge is true, allocations are purged\n   141→func DeregisterJob(jobName string, purge bool) error {\n   142→    client, err := newNomadClient()\n   143→    if err != nil { return err }\n   144→    _, _, err = client.Jobs().Deregister(jobName, purge, nil)\n   145→    if err != nil {\n   146→        return fmt.Errorf(\"deregister job: %w\", err)\n   147→    }\n   148→    return nil\n   149→}\n   150→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

   280→        "content": "     1→package transflow\n     2→\n     3→import (\n     4→\t\"context\"\n     5→\t\"time\"\n     6→)\n     7→\n     8→// Job submission types for transflow healing workflow\n     9→\n    10→// JobSpec describes a job to be submitted to the orchestrator\n    11→type JobSpec struct {\n    12→\tName    string                 `json:\"name\"`\n    13→\tType    string                 `json:\"type\"`\n    14→\tHCLPath string                 `json:\"hcl_path\"`\n    15→\tEnvVars map[string]string      `json:\"env_vars\"`\n    16→\tTimeout time.Duration
